_meta.notes: Bank bonuses are pure cash plays with their own rules: ChexSystems sensitivity, prior-bonus lockouts, 1099-INT for ≥$10. Fewer 5/24 implications since most use soft pulls — but Chase Business Checking is a hard pull and counts.

active_bonuses.lockout: Not received Chase personal checking bonus in past 2 years AND no Chase personal checking open within 90 days
Not received bonus in past 2 years
active_bonuses.notes: Often $750 or higher with elevated offers. Hard pull is the cost; SP version sometimes available via banker.

active_bonuses.lockout: Not opened Citi personal deposit in past 180 days
active_bonuses.notes: Tiered up to $5K bonus for $3M new money. ROI/hour is excellent if you have parkable cash.

active_bonuses.msr_or_dd: Two qualifying DDs of $250+ within 90 days
active_bonuses.lockout: Not had BoA personal checking in past 12 months

active_bonuses.notes: Lowest-effort bank bonus available. Frequently elevated to $200+.

definitely_works: 1099 from broker (interest, dividends in some cases)
depends_on_bank: ACH push from external bank (Chase: NO, Citi: YES, BoA: depends, Fidelity: YES)
Zelle (almost never counts as of 2024-2026 — banks tightened)

1099_int_rules.rule: Banks file 1099-INT for any account paying ≥$10 in interest OR bonus during a tax year. Bonuses count as taxable income.
1099_int_rules.applies_to_state: Yes — varies by state. Some states (CA, OR) treat as interest income; others as misc income.
1099_int_rules.notes: Always factor effective tax rate into bonus value. A $500 bonus at 32% federal + 9% state = $295 net. Doesn't mean don't chase — just means real ROI is ~60% of headline for high earners.
